page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the n2 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor operating at 2.6 GHz, featuring 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The instance is configured with 96 GB of dedicated system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25%. The Intel Xeon processor delivers top-tier performance in Passmark CPU encryption tests, aided by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES. Single-core performance remains in the middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth is robust, with large-block read-only speeds reaching 318.89 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-highcpu-96 maximizes compute density for parallelizable workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The primary tradeoff is the constrained memory capacity of 1 GB per vCPU, which makes it unsuitable for memory-intensive databases or large-scale in-memory data processing. It is highly suited for CPU-bound tasks such as software compilation, ray tracing, cryptography, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 96 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 48 physical cores. Designed for high-density compute workloads, it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, offering a lean 1.0 GB of memory per vCPU. Benchmarks show strong multi-core performance, placing in the top 25% for multi-threaded operations, and top-tier results in encryption tasks due to AVX-512 support. While single-core speeds and memory capacity are moderate, the instance provides excellent resource density for parallel processing. It is best suited for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, cryptography, ray tracing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and a 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server designed for parallel processing, cryptographic operations, and multi-threaded workloads without the overhead of excess memory.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 96 GB RAM powered by a 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon processor
Strong multi-core performance and top-tier cryptographic execution
Low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.0 GB per core for compute-focused efficiency
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving
